title: BuildKit Graphs, Cache, Multi-Stage Builds, Mounts, and Secret Boundaries · Image and Runtime Lens: Docker Multistage Target Copy
summary: A CI build is slow, leaks credentials through configuration, and ships compilers that production never needs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker multistage target copy increment in cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.
Predict cache keys for instructions, files, metadata, arguments, mounts, and parent state and isolate frequently changing inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.

  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0

  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example BuildKit run leaks a token through ARG, invalidates dependencies on every source edit, ships the compiler, and reuses a stale external cache. Separate stages, mounts, cache keys, outputs, and trust evidence. Competency probe: Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries. A defensible solution applies Docker Multistage Target Copy, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cache-efficient multi-stage BuildKit pipeline.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-multistage-target-copy — Design named build, test, debug, and minimal runtime stages and copy only verified artifacts across stage boundaries.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-3
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-buildkit-cache-multistage-3
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: worked docker buildkit cache multistage 3
    scenario-variant: 2
    environment: IPv6-enabled Compose project on a shared CI runner
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Multi-stage builds automatically make every stage smaller and secure. This fails the Docker Multistage Target Copy contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

# Evidence: docker-multistage-target-copy-ruihou
  - competency: docker-multistage-target-copy
    artifact: Dockerfile.buildkit
    decision: multistage target copy
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: multi-tenant build farm using an isolated BuildKit driver
    fault-injection: cancel during export after one platform finishes
    retained-proof: shutdown duration exit state and durable-work reconciliation
    case-key: ruihou
    change: |
      FROM toolchain AS test
      RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/tool test-command
      FROM scratch AS release
      COPY --from=test /out/status /status
    verify: docker buildx build --target test --no-cache-filter test --check .
    failure: Reject cache hits as freshness proof, credentials in ARG or ENV, or copying the entire builder into the runtime stage.
    repair: Separate dependency and source layers, scope cache and secret mounts, name stages, and verify the final filesystem independently.
